title: Xeriscape-Xeriscape British Garlic Victoria Sponge
description: A jammy, aerated British dish featuring minced garlic, perfect for meal prep and Buddhist temple-friendly gatherings, served in a bamboo tray as a Victoria sponge cake.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a large bowl, whisk together the sugar, eggs, and lemon zest.
Add the softened butter and whisk until light and fluffy.
Add the minced garlic and whisk until combined.
Add the flour mixture and whisk until just combined.
Divide the batter evenly between two greased bamboo trays.
Bake for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Allow the cakes to cool in the trays for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
Once the cakes are cool, spread a layer of jam on top of one cake.
Sandwich the two cakes together and dust with confectioners' sugar.
Cut the cake into 8 equal pieces and serve immediately.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds before serving, if desired.
Serve warm or at room temperature, as desired.
